page: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-800s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 8 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-CPU ratio. It is powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz across 8 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation. The hardware includes 800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core testing placing the server in the top 10% of its class. Multi-core performance and PassMark CPU scores align with average mid-tier expectations. Memory performance is strong, with uncached large-block read and write bandwidths ranking in the top 25%, supported by a PassMark memory score that also falls in the top 25%. Database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput consistently register in the average range.

This server offers a high memory-to-vCPU density, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. However, the shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, predictable multi-core performance. Given the 64.0 GB RAM capacity and 800 GB NVMe SSD, this configuration is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, memory-intensive data processing, and general-purpose web serving.

Performance benchmarks reveal top-tier single-core CPU capabilities alongside strong memory read and write bandwidths, while multi-core execution and database operations perform at average levels. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy operations. This configuration is suitable for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive web serving workloads.
